Print Sheet: Underworld: Gangs, Police Informants, Sex Workers. Elite: LAPD, LA Coroner's Office
Dexterity - (Burst of Speed, Disarm, Fighting Blind, or Quick Draw)
Charisma - +3 Social on Non Power uses dealing with focus.
Wits - +3 Mental on Non Power uses dealing with focus.
7/5/3 traits, 3/2/1 backgrounds, 2/1/1 disciplines, 4/3/3/2/2/2/1/1/1/1 abilities, 30pts freebie, 7pts merits max, 7pts flaws max - All Merit/Flaw/Freebies must be on XP Spends Sheet

records.what_was_bought: If you need to add more cells for character creation. Go to row 18 and add from there to make sure none of the forumulas break.
* Earned XP in Game / Convention Game / 6 Game Bonus XP / What Was Bought *

rows: The day you graduated from the police academy was the proudest day of your life. You knew the job would be hard, but you were ready for anything. However, as you earned professional recognition and promotions, you soon began to suspect that the LAPD was not the bastion of civic stability you always thought of it as. Sure, there were always bad cops who got caught and punished. But you begin to notice... things. Small irregularities, covered up. Street closures for fabricated reasons. Things going missing from evidence lockers and no one caring. *Bodies* going missing from the morgue, and no one caring! You pushed these suspicions to the back of your head, however, and focused on doing the best job you could to protect the people of Los Angeles. Someone must have approved of your work, for you were quickly promoted to captain, the youngest woman ever in the history of the LAPD. Then, in August of 2016, your world exploded. Starting with an outbreak of violence in the San Fernando Valley, then slowly moving south, Los Angeles was thrown into chaos for a full week. You didn't sleep, you lived on coffee and cold pizza while trying to contain the worst of it and protect the civilians of Los Angeles. And again, you noticed irregularities. Departures from procedure. Then you actually *saw* the fighting, and all the impossible things that shouldn't have been possible but were. The choice was to kill you or bring you over, a choice you never got to make - it was made for you. During the worst fighting you've ever seen, in Torrance, you took several bullets that would have been enough to end your life if an archon hadn't been nearby. He'd been following your career, impressed that you were smart enough to figure things out and even smarter to keep your mouth shut about it. So you were rewarded with Embrace. Just your luck, however, that your sire was killed the next night in Long Beach. His former allies have helped you, however - they've taught you what you need to know as a new Kindred.
Your relationship to these people is described. This may mean they do not know you.

You don't know Hale's first name. 'archon' seems to be some kind of title. He's told you that your sire was probably one o the archons who died, and has said he'll help you as much as he can. He's given you the phone numbers of someone called a Scourge and someone he says is a Sheriff.
You're told he's the 'Sheriff' of Los Angeles, and a good person to work for. If you can impress him, perhaps he'll help you tmake the transition from human to Kindred.

A Scourge, whatever that means. But more than that. At last, all the weirdness in Los Angeles makes sense, and has a name. You know that being on the good side of important people is the key to survival in this new world, and if anyone is powerful, it's him.

Since no one knows who your sire was, no one knows your clan. Hale has said that, given the circumstances of your Embrace, it may take some time for the Blood to settle. Before that happens, it's imperative you discover your clan identity. You don't know precisely what 'Caitiff' means, only that it's bad and you don't want to be one. If you can't find out who your sire was, convince another clan to adopt you.
Find a place where you can fit in, a job you can do. You still have contacts in the LAPD, and are good at dealing with criminals and petty bureaucracy. Find a way to make these skills work for you.
Though you've been told it's mandatory, you can't forget your old life, your old family. Check in in on them from time to time, make sure they're okay without you.
